previous_statement: (Remark: Inactive constraints reduce effective m) If a conic constraint is inactive at x (i.e. Φ_i(x) - a_i lies in the interior of W_i^+), then any perturbation D preserves this constraint for small |t|, regardless of Φ_i(D). Thus inactive constraints do not contribute to the kernel condition. If k constraints are inactive, then d_F ≤ m' where m' = Σ_{i: active} dim(W_i).
new_statement: (Remark: Inactive constraints reduce effective m) If a conic constraint is inactive at x (i.e. Φ_i(x) - a_i in interior of W_i^+), then face_{W_i^+}(S_i) = W_i^+, so d_F(S_i) = dim(W_i) and the inactive constraint contribution cancels in the slack bound (node 1.16). More precisely, if k constraints are inactive, the slack formulation gives d_F ≤ m' where m' = Σ_{i: active} dim(W_i) - Σ_{i: active} d_F(S_i) ≤ Σ_{i: active} dim(W_i).
